items.text:
1. `_render_iter(entity, content_doc) -> str` helper in `workstream.py`:
generates NNN.md from a state entity + content doc. Format mirrors
current NNN.md structure (frontmatter, heading, DoD, self-review,
handback, verdict stamps from entity.verdicts).
(Evidence: test asserts rendered file matches expected sections.)

2. `open_iteration()` creates `iterations/NNN.json` (validated against
`iteration_content` schema) with DoD skeleton seeded from type-specific
prompts; `dod_artifact` in state entity set to `"iterations/NNN.json"`.
NNN.md immediately regenerated as a view.
(Evidence: after `lume open`, NNN.json exists and validates; NNN.md
contains the skeleton items.)

3. `transition()` no longer calls `frontmatter.parse(iter_path.read_text())`.
Phase and verdict updates go to state.json only. NNN.md regenerated from
NNN.json + updated state entity. Verdict stamps rendered into view only.
(Evidence: grep shows no `frontmatter.parse` call in `transition`;
test shows NNN.md updated after transition.)

4. `frontmatter` import in `workstream.py` removed (now only needed in
`iteration.py` for the migration path).
(Evidence: grep shows no `from . import frontmatter` in workstream.py.)

5. `migrate.py` extended: for each workstream's existing NNN.md files,
parse content sections (DoD preamble + checklist items, self-review,
handback) into NNN.json; update `dod_artifact` in state.json. Idempotent.
(Evidence: running `lume migrate` twice produces identical NNN.json files.)

6. All three existing workstreams migrated: `iterations/NNN.json` present
for each iteration, NNN.md regenerated as a view, state.json
`dod_artifact` updated. No machine field read from NNN.md by the engine.
(Evidence: `lume migrate` run; grep confirms no NNN.md reads in workstream.py.)

7. Full suite green; ≥ 6 new tests: NNN.json created on open, dod_artifact
points to NNN.json, transition re-renders NNN.md from NNN.json, no
frontmatter parse in transition, migrate produces valid NNN.json,
migrate is idempotent. No tests removed.
(Evidence: test run 0 failures.)

dod.preamble:
Replace `iterations/NNN.md` as the authored source for iteration content with a
schema-validated `iterations/NNN.json`. NNN.md becomes a derived view,
regenerated by the engine on every mutation. No machine field is read from
NNN.md after this change.

Content schema (`iteration_content`): `{id, dod: {preamble, items[]}, self_review, handback}`.
Verdict stamps remain in `state.json` (done in P5); this change captures
the prose content.

Changes confined to `iteration.py`, `workstream.py`, and `migrate.py`.

handback:
Ready for review. Key changes:
- iteration.py: renamed _SKELETONS -> SKELETONS; added parse_dod_items, render_iter_md, _ITEM_LINE_RE; to_entity() changed dod_artifact to .json.
- workstream.py: removed frontmatter import; added SKELETONS, parse_dod_items, render_iter_md imports; added _load_iter_content, _save_iter_content, _render_iter helpers; open_iteration creates NNN.json + renders NNN.md; transition() stripped to load+render, no frontmatter.parse.
- migrate.py: added _extract_section, _parse_iter_content, migrate_iterations; migrate_all calls it; all three workstreams migrated.
- Test helpers in transitions/workstream_open/cli/state_flip/discovery_verbs updated to create NNN.json alongside NNN.md.

Verify: grep -n frontmatter .lume/engine/lume/workstream.py returns only a comment; to_entity() returns .json; 27 NNN.json files on disk.
self_review:
All 7 DoD items complete. The triple pattern from P10 (_load_*, _save_*, _render_*) extends cleanly to iterations: _load_iter_content, _save_iter_content, _render_iter. workstream.py no longer imports or calls frontmatter at all. transition() is now 6 lines instead of 25.

Migration works correctly for all content types: multi-line DoD items are captured via a continuation-line accumulator; self-review and handback prose are stripped of placeholder text before saving. Running lume migrate on the live workstreams produced 27 NNN.json files (6 + 8 + 13), all schema-valid.

The accept verdict reason bug was caught by the test suite: the original transition() recorded the note in state for accept (since VERDICT_LABELS includes accept), but the old stamp-line code only rendered it for reject. After P11, render_iter_md renders ALL verdicts from state, so the hidden reason appeared. Fixed at the source: transition() now only sets reason for reject.

212 tests passing. 17 new tests in test_iteration_json.py.
